edges.evidence_quote: On 4 October 2009, several Hells Angels and allied Red Devils performed a raid on an Outlaws clubhouse in Kortrijk.
edges.context: Hells Angels raided Outlaws clubhouse, part of territorial dispute.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws entered Canada on 1 July 1977 when a number of Satan's Choice chapters under the leadership of Garnet "Mother" McEwen "patched over" to join the Outlaws.
edges.context: Satan's Choice chapters patched over to become Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Lobos and the Chosen Few gangs against the Outlaws.
edges.context: Lobos allied with Satan's Choice and Chosen Few against Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Lobos and the Chosen Few gangs against the Outlaws.
edges.context: Chosen Few allied with Satan's Choice and Lobos against Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1984, the Outlaws entered Toronto when their national president, Stanley "Beamer" McConnery, persuaded the Iron Hawgs gang to "patch over" to the Outlaws.
edges.context: Iron Hawgs patched over to become Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: Hulko's funeral was held in Sault Ste. Marie on 18 August 2002 and conducted entirely by members of the Outlaws and their support club, the Black Pistons.
edges.context: Black Pistons are a support club for Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: On 8 July 2023, members of the Loners and Outlaws were involved in an altercation in Cornwall which police believe was targeted.
edges.context: Outlaws and Loners clashed in an altercation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws entered Finland via Russia, when a feeder club called Fifteen MC South Karelia was established in Lappeenranta in 2012 with support from a Russian Outlaws chapter.
edges.context: Fifteen MC was a feeder club for Outlaws in Finland.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws expanded into the United Kingdom in February 2000 after amalgamating the Midland Outlaws, an independent club which had been in existence since 1992.
edges.context: Outlaws amalgamated with Midland Outlaws to enter UK.

edges.evidence_quote: The first Welsh Outlaws chapter was formed in 1993 following a "patch over" of the Henchmen MC, which was founded in North Wales in 1969.
edges.context: Henchmen MC patched over to become Outlaws chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: The West Wales chapter originated in as the Strays MC in 1979.
edges.context: Strays MC became the West Wales Outlaws chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws entered Georgia when the Iron Cross gang of Atlanta "patched over" on October 31, 1970
edges.context: The Iron Cross gang patched over to become part of the Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: Frank Rego Vital of Roberta, an Outlaws member, was shot and killed in an early morning gunfight on June 24, 2007, in the parking lot of The Crazy Horse Saloon strip club in Forest Park by two members of the Renegades MC
edges.context: Members of the Renegades MC shot and killed an Outlaws member in a gunfight.

edges.evidence_quote: The North Side chapter was initiated via the absorption of the Wheelmen Motorcycle Club in 1993
edges.context: The Outlaws absorbed the Wheelmen Motorcycle Club to form their North Side chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: The West Side chapter was formed in 1994 when the Outlaws amalgamated the High Spirits Motorcycle Club in Bensenville
edges.context: The Outlaws amalgamated the High Spirits Motorcycle Club to form their West Side chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1991, the club also initiated its "Stateline" chapter in Antioch by absorbing the Booze Runners Motorcycle Club
edges.context: The Outlaws absorbed the Booze Runners to form their Stateline chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws are also affiliated with the Menard Brotherhood, a "prison biker gang" operating in the Illinois state prison system
edges.context: The Outlaws are affiliated with the Menard Brotherhood.

edges.evidence_quote: In November 2022, four members of the Outlaws were shot and injured during a confrontation with members of the Mongols at a bar in the Archer Heights area of Chicago
edges.context: Outlaws and Mongols members had a violent confrontation in Chicago.

edges.evidence_quote: The coalition between the Outlaws and the Outfit, known as the "Double O Alliance", began as far back as early 1975
edges.context: The Outlaws and Chicago Outfit formed the Double O Alliance, cooperating in vice rackets and later gambling and burglary.

edges.evidence_quote: On September 18, 1987, Outlaws were involved in a brawl with two Hells Angels members in a bar outside Joliet which left Cleveland Hells Angels chapter president Kenneth "Kenny" Yates shot in the foot and robbed of his club "colors"
edges.context: Outlaws and Hells Angels engaged in a brawl and shooting.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws attempted to coerce the Hell's Henchmen, one of the oldest and largest motorcycle gangs in the Chicago area, into "patching over" to join the Outlaws
edges.context: Outlaws attempted to coerce Hell's Henchmen to join them, leading to conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On November 13, 1990, the Booze Runners, a gang founded and led by Kevin "Spike" O'Neill which was vying for chapter status in the Outlaws, bombed the Hell's Henchmen clubhouse in Rockford
edges.context: Booze Runners bombed Hell's Henchmen clubhouse as part of Outlaws' conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The chapter also recruited several former members of the Night Sinners, a defunct biker gang in Waukegan
edges.context: Outlaws recruited former members of the Night Sinners.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels decided to absorb the Hell's Henchmen in order to gain a foothold in the large and lucrative drug trade in the Chicago area
edges.context: Hells Angels absorbed Hell's Henchmen to expand into Chicago.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws then tried to kill Roger Fiebrantz, Mathias' successor as Rockford Hell's Henchmen president.
edges.context: Outlaws carried out bombings and murder attempts against Hell's Henchmen members.

edges.evidence_quote: Castle's murder was the final act of violence in the biker war between the Outlaws and the Hells Angels.
edges.context: Outlaws and Hells Angels were engaged in a biker war, including murders and bombings.

edges.evidence_quote: members of the Hell's Henchmen partied with Hells Angels at bars in Chicago, Calumet City and Alsip.
edges.context: Hell's Henchmen socialized with Hells Angels and later agreed to prospect for them.

edges.evidence_quote: the Outlaws planned to use a 'war wagon'... to attack members of the Invaders at the speedway.
edges.context: Outlaws planned an attack on Invaders, who disbanded afterward.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws merged with the Gypsy Raiders motorcycle gang of Louisville in August 1964.
edges.context: Outlaws merged with Gypsy Raiders.

edges.evidence_quote: A gunfight between members of Outlaws, the Animals, and the Invaders resulted in an Invaders biker being shot.
edges.context: Outlaws were in a gunfight with Animals and Invaders.

edges.evidence_quote: A violent rivalry between the Outlaws and the Iron Horsemen in Greater Cincinnati began in the late 1960s.
edges.context: Outlaws had a violent rivalry with Iron Horsemen, including bombings.

edges.evidence_quote: The Youngstown Outlaws chapter has been associated with the Pittsburgh crime family in contract killing, witness intimidation, debt collection, drug dealing, arson and assaults.
edges.context: Youngstown Outlaws chapter cooperated with Pittsburgh crime family in various criminal activities.

edges.evidence_quote: On May 18, 1975, Invaders member Billy J. Schmidt was killed and four others were wounded during a gun battle at the Fugueros clubhouse in Middletown that also involved members of the Outlaws, the Iron Horsemen and the Saints.
edges.context: Outlaws and Invaders were involved in a gun battle together, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The conflict between the Outlaws and the Hells Angels resulted in at least twelve homicides in Northeast Ohio in the years leading up to 1986.
edges.context: Outlaws and Iron Horsemen have a history of conflict, including assaults and a shootout.

edges.evidence_quote: On May 18, 1975, Invaders member Billy J. Schmidt was killed and four others were wounded during a gun battle at the Fugueros clubhouse in Middletown that also involved members of the Outlaws, the Iron Horsemen and the Saints.
edges.context: Outlaws and Saints were involved in a gun battle together, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The conflict between the Outlaws and the Hells Angels resulted in at least twelve homicides in Northeast Ohio in the years leading up to 1986.
edges.context: Outlaws and Hells Angels are rivals engaged in a violent conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: According to law enforcement, the Outlaws were in the process of recruiting the Freeboarders in order to gain a foothold in Cincinnati, which provoked retaliation from the Iron Horsemen.
edges.context: Outlaws were recruiting Freeboarders as an affiliated club.

edges.evidence_quote: On February 24, 2001, several members of the Dayton Outlaws chapter, along with members of the Avenger Motorcycle Club, visited Spanky's Dollhouse...
edges.context: Outlaws and Avenger members were together in a fight, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws expanded into Virginia in 2006 after reaching an accord with the Pagan's.
edges.context: Outlaws and Pagans had an accord allowing Outlaws expansion into Virginia.

edges.evidence_quote: In September 2008, a group of over fifty Outlaw and Pagan bikers prepared to confront members of the Merciless Souls Motorcycle Cub, a gang affiliated with the Hells Angels, at Virginia Motorsports Park in Dinwiddie.
edges.context: Outlaws prepared to confront Merciless Souls, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On March 14, 2009, Outlaws and Pagan's members lured members of the Hells Angels and the Desperados Motorcycle Club into an ambush...
edges.context: Outlaws ambushed Desperados, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The American Outlaws Association "patched over" the Gypsy Outlaws gang of Milwaukee on July 4, 1964.
edges.context: Outlaws absorbed Gypsy Outlaws by patching them over.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1992, around forty members of the Outlaws crashed a party being held by the Milwaukee-based Sinners Motorcycle Club and thereafter began pressuring the Sinners to wear an "A.O.A." patch on their vests to show allegiance to the Outlaws.
edges.context: Outlaws pressured Sinners to show allegiance, leading to conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In September 2008, a group of over fifty Outlaw and Pagan bikers prepared to confront members of the Merciless Souls Motorcycle Cub, a gang affiliated with the Hells Angels, at Virginia Motorsports Park in Dinwiddie.
edges.context: Merciless Souls are affiliated with Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: On March 14, 2009, Outlaws and Pagan's members lured members of the Hells Angels and the Desperados Motorcycle Club into an ambush...
edges.context: Pagans cooperated with Outlaws to ambush Hells Angels, indicating conflict between Pagans and Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: On March 14, 2009, Outlaws and Pagan's members lured members of the Hells Angels and the Desperados Motorcycle Club into an ambush...
edges.context: Pagans cooperated with Outlaws to ambush Desperados, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On March 14, 2009, Outlaws and Pagan's members lured members of the Hells Angels and the Desperados Motorcycle Club into an ambush...
edges.context: Hells Angels and Desperados were both ambushed together, suggesting cooperation.
